# vibez.surf

> A free, browser-based music visualizer and rhythm game. Users pick any audio file (MP3, WAV, FLAC, OGG, AAC), paste a Suno AI share link, or generate a track via Suno; the site analyzes the waveform and builds a 3D ribbon track they ride to the beat, collecting glowing blocks placed on musical-energy peaks.

vibez.surf runs entirely in the browser — no install, no accounts for the core experience. It is inspired by the 2008 Windows game "Audiosurf" by Dylan Fitterer, reimplemented as a web app with vanilla TypeScript + Three.js.

## What the user does

- Drops an audio file into the picker, OR pastes a direct audio URL / Suno `cdn1.suno.ai/<uuid>.mp3` link / Suno `/song/<uuid>` page URL / Suno `/s/<code>` share link.
- Optionally, types a prompt and uses the built-in Suno AI integration to generate a new track (requires the user to paste their own Suno session cookie — BYOK, no shared account).
- Steers with arrow keys / A-D / touch swipe.
- Aims to collect glowing blocks placed on the beat; misses cost points.
- On finish, a scorecard shows hit / missed / total. Recently played songs are saved locally for one-tap replay.

## How the game works

- `decodeAudioData` on the file bytes produces a PCM buffer.
- A pool of Web Workers runs 4096-sample FFT windows across the interleaved stream to extract per-chunk intensities and a full spectrum.
- Low-band (~20 Hz) and high-band (~7500 Hz) beat indices place the blocks.
- Per-chunk intensity drives track speed, hue (HSV), rocket-fire size, and the height of side hexagon pillars.
- A cubic uniform B-spline polyline (built from the intensity signal) forms the track centerline; a second, more heavily smoothed signal drives horizontal sweeps.
- The ship's position is `audio.currentTime / duration` mapped to spline `t` — so the game is perfectly locked to playback. No desync, no physics.
- Only postprocessing is a single UnrealBloomPass.

## Stack

- TypeScript, Vite, Three.js, Web Audio API, Web Workers, Vitest.
- No framework (no React/Vue). UI is vanilla DOM in `src/ui/*`.
- ESM only, ES2022 target.

## Credits and links

- Inspired by: Audiosurf (Dylan Fitterer, 2008).
- Suno generation uses a self-hosted [gcui-art/suno-api](https://github.com/gcui-art/suno-api) fork (BYOK model: each visitor supplies their own Suno session cookie).
- Suno share-link resolution: a small Cloudflare Worker in `workers/suno-share-resolver/`.
- Repo: https://github.com/donth77/vibez.surf
- Author: Tom Donohue (https://github.com/donth77)

## Non-goals

- Not a Suno front-end, not a music-streaming service, and not affiliated with Suno, Audiosurf, or Dylan Fitterer.
- Does not store uploaded audio anywhere except the user's local browser (IndexedDB, under a 10-file FIFO cap).
- Does not track, log, or forward user prompts or cookies anywhere except the vibez.surf-operated proxy (which also does not persist them).

## How to play

[Play vibez.surf](https://vibez.surf/)
